prior.title: ICAP ETS Map
prior.summary: ACTIVE DATA SOURCE. Updated continuously. ACTION NOW: Map carbon pricing exposure across all trade lanes. Include jurisdiction-specific carbon costs in rate modelling. Monitor India Carbon Credit Trading Scheme and Brazil market development. Owner: Finance + Strategy.
prior.what_is_it: The International Carbon Action Partnership (ICAP) ETS Map provides a comprehensive database of emissions trading systems worldwide — 38 systems in force, 11 under development as of 2026. Tracks allowance prices, coverage, and design features for every active carbon market. The primary comparative reference for understanding carbon cost exposure across jurisdictions.
prior.why_matters: Carbon market prices determine surcharge levels on freight in each jurisdiction. For freight forwarders: (1) compare ETS costs across trade lanes — EU ETS ~€68-81/tCO₂ vs UK ETS ~£35-50 vs Singapore S$25/tCO₂ vs Korea ₩10,000-15,000, (2) new carbon markets emerging in India, Brazil, Mexico mean new cost layers ahead, (3) CBAM links EU import costs to origin-country carbon pricing — lower carbon prices at origin = higher CBAM cost at EU border.
key_data: Singapore carbon tax: S$25/tCO₂ (rising to S$50-80 by 2030)
Coverage: ~23% of global GHG emissions under some carbon pricing
prior.reasoning: Most detailed ETS status tracker globally — covers all operational and planned schemes.
prior.full_brief:
# Purpose and Scope of This Document

**Format:** Regulatory Fact Document
**Primary Source:** ICAP ETS Map, International Carbon Action Partnership (https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets), data current as of 2025–2026 reporting cycle.
**Date of this brief:** April 2026

This document synthesises the global emissions trading system (ETS) landscape as mapped by the International Carbon Action Partnership (ICAP), filtered through the operational profile of a freight forwarder moving high-value, time-sensitive cargo — live events, fine art, luxury goods, film and TV, high-value automotive, and humanitarian goods — across the Americas, Europe, and Asia, with air as the primary mode, road secondary, and ocean tertiary.

**Convention notes:**
- All prices are as reported by ICAP for 2025 unless otherwise noted. Currency conversions are ICAP's own published figures.
- "The workspace" refers throughout to the freight forwarding operation described in the profile. No company or individual is named.
- Items requiring legal interpretation are labeled **Legal Confirmation Required**.
- Operator-level interpretations are labeled as such and do not constitute legal authority.

---

# What This Regulation Is and Why It Applies to the Workspace

## What the ICAP ETS Map Is

The ICAP ETS Map is the primary authoritative global registry of emissions trading systems. It is published by the International Carbon Action Partnership, an intergovernmental forum founded in 2007 by government representatives from jurisdictions that have implemented or are planning to implement ETS. The map tracks 57 ETS globally: **41 in force, 7 under development, and 9 under consideration** as of the 2025–2026 data cycle.

*Source: ICAP ETS Map, International Carbon Action Partnership, 2025–2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets.*

## Why This Applies to a Freight Forwarder

ETS systems directly impose compliance costs on carriers — ocean, air, and road — operating within covered jurisdictions. Those compliance costs are passed downstream as surcharges to forwarders and, ultimately, to shippers. A freight forwarder operating across Europe, the Americas, and Asia is therefore exposed to ETS-driven cost increases on every mode, on every lane that crosses a jurisdiction with a live or developing ETS.

The workspace's cargo verticals — live events, fine art, luxury goods, film and TV, high-value automotive, and humanitarian — are characteristically air-dependent, time-sensitive, and frequently cross-border. These characteristics concentrate ETS exposure in the highest-cost mode (air, via EU ETS aviation scope) and in the highest-cost jurisdictions (EU, UK, California, Washington State). The workspace does not hold ETS compliance obligations directly as a forwarder; its exposure is cost pass-through from carriers. **Legal Confirmation Required: whether any jurisdiction in scope imposes direct ETS reporting or registration obligations on freight forwarders as distinct from carriers.**

*Source: ICAP ETS Map, International Carbon Action Partnership, 2025–2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets.*

---

# Issues Requiring Immediate Action

**COST ALERT** — EU ETS aviation surcharges are live and carrier-pass-through is already occurring on intra-EEA and departing flights. For a workspace routing live events, fine art, and luxury goods primarily by air across Europe, every air shipment touching an EU ETS-covered aviation segment carries an embedded carbon cost. The workspace must establish whether carriers are itemising this surcharge separately or absorbing it into base rate. If it is not itemised, the workspace cannot report it to clients or reclaim it in commercial discussions.

- **Action:** Within 30 days, request from each air carrier used on Europe trade lanes a breakdown of whether EU ETS aviation costs are included in base rate or quoted as a separate line item. Document the response.
- **Who is affected:** All cargo verticals moving by air through the EU/EEA, primarily live events, fine art, luxury goods, film and TV.
- **Why now:** EU ETS aviation scope has been in force since 2012 for intra-EEA flights; full-scope flights and maritime were expanded. Carrier pass-through is active. Not knowing the embedded cost means the workspace cannot price, report, or negotiate accurately.

*Source: ICAP ETS Map — EU ETS entry, ICAP, 2025–2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets.*

---

**COST ALERT** — EU ETS maritime scope is in force and expanding. Ocean shipments touching EU ports carry EU ETS costs for vessel operators. For the workspace's tertiary ocean volume (e.g., heavy automotive, large-format live events equipment), carriers on EU port-calling routes are surrendering allowances, and cost pass-through is occurring industry-wide.

- **Action:** Within 30 days, confirm with each ocean carrier on Europe trade lanes whether EU ETS maritime surcharges are itemised on invoices. If not itemised, flag for commercial and finance teams.
- **Who is affected:** High-value automotive and large-format live events cargo moved by ocean to/from EU ports.
- **Why now:** EU ETS maritime entered Phase 1 in 2024 (50% of emissions on qualifying voyages). Carrier pass-through is in market. This is not a future cost; it is a current one.

*Source: ICAP ETS Map — EU ETS entry, ICAP, 2025–2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets.*

---

**MONITORING** — California Cap-and-Trade and Washington Cap-and-Invest apply to fuel distributors supplying transport fuel on those trade lanes. These costs flow through to road carriers and, on some ocean routes, vessel operators bunkering in-state. For the workspace's Americas road movements (e.g., last-mile delivery of high-value automotive or live events equipment in California or Washington), embedded carbon costs are present in trucking rates.

- **Action:** No immediate compliance action required. Flag for rate renegotiation cycle: ask road carriers serving California and Washington whether carbon cost pass-through is embedded in line-haul rates.
- **Who is affected:** High-value automotive, live events cargo moving by road in California or Washington.
- **Why now:** California program has been in force since 2013 (current allowance price USD 29.10 in 2025); Washington since 2023 (current price USD 61.36 in 2025 — the highest sub-national price in the Americas). These are current costs, not anticipated ones.

*Source: ICAP ETS Map — USA California and USA Washington entries, ICAP, 2025–2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets.*

---

**MONITORING** — Japan GX-ETS covers maritime, domestic aviation, and transport sectors and is in force. The workspace's Japan-routed air and road movements face carrier-level compliance costs under this system.

- **Action:** In the next 60 days, request Japan-operating air and road carriers to confirm whether GX-ETS costs are passed through in rates on Japan trade lanes.
- **Who is affected:** Live events, luxury goods, fine art, film and TV cargo transiting Japan by air or road.
- **Why now:** GX-ETS is in force. Specific allowance price not published in available ICAP data for this entry; effect on the workspace's Japan corridor requires carrier-specific data.

*Source: ICAP ETS Map — Japan GX-ETS entry, ICAP, 2025–2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets.*

---

**MONITORING** — Korea ETS (K-ETS) covers maritime, domestic aviation, transport, and other sectors. Korea is a significant trade lane for luxury goods and high-value automotive. Allowance price in 2025: KRW 9,393 (USD 6.60) on secondary market.

- **Action:** Monitor K-ETS pricing cycle. At next carrier contract review for Korea routes, confirm pass-through status.
- **Who is affected:** Luxury goods, high-value automotive on Asia (Korea) lanes.

*Source: ICAP ETS Map — Korea ETS entry, ICAP, 2025–2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets.*

---

**MONITORING** — EU ETS 2 (covering road transport and buildings) is under development, covering all 30 EU/EEA member states. If enacted as proposed, it will impose costs directly on road fuel distributors serving EU trucking, feeding through to road carrier rates across the workspace's European road movements.

- **Action:** Track EU ETS 2 legislative progress. No compliance action required now, but road carrier contracts in Europe should include a carbon cost review clause before EU ETS 2 enters force.
- **Who is affected:** All cargo verticals using road transport within the EU.

*Source: ICAP ETS Map — EU ETS 2 entry, ICAP, 2025–2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets.*

---

# How the Workspace Sits in the Compliance Chain

## Direct Compliance Obligations

The workspace operates as a freight forwarder. In all ETS systems tracked by ICAP, compliance obligations — allowance purchase, surrender, and MRV reporting — sit with the regulated entity: the vessel operator (maritime ETS), the aircraft operator (aviation ETS), or the fuel distributor/industrial operator (transport fuel ETS). The workspace is not a vessel operator, aircraft operator, or fuel distributor and therefore does not hold direct ETS surrender or registration obligations under any system listed in the ICAP map.

**Legal Confirmation Required:** Whether any current or developing ETS in the workspace's operating jurisdictions (EU, UK, US states, Japan, Korea, Australia, New Zealand) imposes direct registration, reporting, or data-collection obligations on freight forwarders as intermediaries, distinct from carriers. No such obligation is documented in the ICAP ETS Map source data; this gap should be confirmed with legal counsel.

## Indirect Exposure: Cost Pass-Through

The workspace's ETS exposure is entirely cost pass-through from carriers:

- **Air carriers** subject to EU ETS aviation scope pass carbon costs through to customers via surcharges or embedded in base rates.
- **Ocean carriers** subject to EU ETS maritime scope pass costs through via bunker adjustment factors or carbon surcharges.
- **Road carriers** subject to transport fuel ETS (Germany national ETS, Austrian ETS, EU ETS 2 when enacted, California, Washington, Québec) pass costs through via fuel surcharges or rate adjustments.

The workspace's commercial exposure depends on whether these costs are visible and itemised in carrier invoices or hidden in base rates. Visibility determines whether the workspace can report, negotiate, or pass through costs to clients accurately.

*Source: ICAP ETS Map, ICAP, 2025–2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets.*

## Client-Facing Exposure

Clients in live events, fine art, luxury goods, film and TV, and high-value automotive increasingly request emissions reporting on their supply chain. Where carrier ETS costs are itemised, the workspace can report embedded carbon pricing as part of emissions cost disclosure. Where they are not itemised, the workspace cannot. This is a data gap, not a compliance gap — but it becomes a commercial gap when clients request it.

---

# Substantive Requirements

*This section documents the ETS obligations that apply to the workspace's carrier ecosystem, filtered by transport mode and trade lane. The workspace does not hold direct compliance obligations; the obligations below sit with carriers. They are documented here because they drive cost pass-through and client-disclosure requirements.*

## Air — Primary Mode | EU ETS Aviation

**Scope:** EU ETS covers domestic aviation within the EEA and, following recent extensions, certain departing/arriving flights. Aircraft operators must surrender EU Allowances (EUAs) for covered emissions.

**Current price:** EUR 74.35 (USD 84.01) average secondary market price in 2025.

**Cap:** 26.2 MtCO2e (2026, aviation).

**Effect on the workspace (air):** Every air carrier operating intra-EEA or on covered routes incurs EUA costs at the current price. These costs are passed to freight customers. For time-sensitive, high-value cargo (live events, fine art, film and TV) where air is non-substitutable, the workspace has no mode-shift lever. The ETS cost is embedded in every EEA air shipment.

*Source: ICAP ETS Map — EU ETS entry, ICAP, 2025–2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets.*

## Air — Japan GX-ETS

**Scope:** Japan GX-ETS covers domestic aviation and transport. In force as of the ICAP data cycle.

**Current allowance price:** Not published in available ICAP data for the GX-ETS entry. Effect on Japan air corridor requires carrier-specific data.

*Source: ICAP ETS Map — Japan GX-ETS entry, ICAP, 2025–2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets.*

## Air — Korea K-ETS

**Scope:** K-ETS covers domestic aviation. In force since 2015.

**Current price:** KRW 9,393 (USD 6.60) secondary market, 2025.

**Effect on the workspace (Korea air):** Modest per-tonne price relative to EU ETS. Pass-through on Korea air routes for luxury goods and automotive cargo is low in absolute terms at current prices but should be tracked as K-ETS cap tightens.

*Source: ICAP ETS Map — Korea ETS entry, ICAP, 2025–2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets.*

## Road — Secondary Mode | Germany National ETS

**Scope:** Germany National ETS covers transport fuel use (road). In force since 2021. Fixed price EUR 55 (USD 62.15) in 2025; transitions to auctioning with price corridor in 2026.

**Total revenue 2025:** EUR 16 billion (USD 18.1 billion) — indicating the scale of cost flowing through the German transport fuel supply chain.

**Effect on the workspace (road in Germany):** Road carriers operating in Germany embed this carbon cost in fuel surcharges or rates. For the workspace's European road movements — particularly high-value automotive and live events requiring dedicated trucking through Germany — this is a current embedded cost.

*Source: ICAP ETS Map — German National ETS entry, ICAP, 2025–2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets.*

## Road — Austria National ETS

**Scope:** Austrian National ETS covers transport fuel use. Fixed price EUR 55 (USD 62) through 2027.

**Effect on the workspace (road in Austria):** Same mechanism as Germany. Pass-through via carrier fuel surcharges on Austria road movements.

*Source: ICAP ETS Map — Austrian National ETS entry, ICAP, 2025–2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets.*

## Road — Québec Cap-and-Trade (Americas)

**Scope:** Québec Cap-and-Trade covers transport fuel. In force since 2013, linked with California.

**Current price:** CAD 48.61 (USD 35.24) weighted average secondary market, 2025.

**Effect on the workspace (road in Québec/Canada):** Relevant for live events or film and TV cargo moving by road in Québec. Pass-through mechanism same as above.

*Source: ICAP ETS Map — Canada Québec entry, ICAP, 2025–2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets.*

## Road — California Cap-and-Trade (Americas)

**Scope:** California Cap-and-Trade covers transport fuel distributors. In force since 2013.

**Current price:** USD 29.10 secondary market, 2025.

**Effect on the workspace (road in California):** Road carriers in California embed this cost. For the workspace's live events, high-value automotive, and luxury goods routed by road in California, this is a current cost embedded in rates.

*Source: ICAP ETS Map — USA California entry, ICAP, 2025–2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets.*

## Road — Washington Cap-and-Invest (Americas)

**Scope:** Washington Cap-and-Invest covers transport and other sectors. In force since 2023.

**Current price:** USD 61.36 secondary market, 2025 — the highest sub-national carbon price in the Americas per ICAP data.

**Effect on the workspace (road in Washington):** Road carriers in Washington State pass through this cost. At USD 61.36/tonne, Washington's carbon price is more than double California's on a per-tonne basis and approaching EU levels, with meaningful impact on trucking rates for workspace cargo moving through the Pacific Northwest.

*Source: ICAP ETS Map — USA Washington entry, ICAP, 2025–2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets.*

## Ocean — Tertiary Mode | EU ETS Maritime

**Scope:** EU ETS was extended to cover maritime from 2024. Vessel operators on qualifying voyages to/from EU ports surrender EUAs. Phase 1 covers 50% of emissions on relevant voyages; Phase 2 increases this to 100%.

**Current price:** EUR 74.35 (USD 84.01) average secondary market, 2025 (same EUA pool as power and industry).

**Cap:** 1,185.4 MtCO2e (2026, covering electricity/heat generation, industrial manufacturing, and maritime transport combined).

**Effect on the workspace (ocean to/from EU):** For high-value automotive or large-format live events equipment moved by ocean to EU ports, carrier EUA costs are being passed through. The effect is proportional to cargo weight and voyage emissions share. Exact per-unit pass-through requires carrier-specific invoice data.

*Source: ICAP ETS Map — EU ETS entry, ICAP, 2025–2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets.*

## Ocean — UK ETS Maritime

**Scope:** UK ETS covers maritime (and domestic aviation, industry, power). In force since 2021.

**Current price:** GBP 50.34 (USD 66.28) secondary market, 2025.

**Cap:** 77.4 MtCO2e (2026).

**Effect on the workspace (ocean via UK ports):** Vessel operators on covered UK voyages incur UK ETS costs. For the workspace's ocean shipments touching UK ports, pass-through applies at GBP 50.34/tonne — close in magnitude to the EU ETS price.

*Source: ICAP ETS Map — UK ETS entry, ICAP, 2025–2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets.*

## Ocean — New Zealand ETS

**Scope:** New Zealand ETS covers maritime, transport, waste, forestry, and other sectors. In force since 2008.

**Current price:** NZD 55.04 (USD 31.99) secondary market, 2025.

**Effect on the workspace (ocean via New Zealand):** Relevant only if the workspace routes ocean cargo via New Zealand. At current volumes for the workspace's described verticals, this is likely a low-frequency exposure; flag for review if New Zealand routes are used for high-value automotive.

*Source: ICAP ETS Map — New Zealand ETS entry, ICAP, 2025–2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets.*

---

# Anticipated Authoritative Guidance and Pending Regulatory Events

**MONITORING** — **EU ETS 2 (Road Transport and Buildings):** Under development as of the ICAP 2025–2026 data cycle. Covers transport fuel across all 30 EU/EEA member states. No implementation date is published in the available ICAP source data. When enacted, EU ETS 2 will add a road transport carbon cost layer on top of existing national systems (Germany, Austria) in those jurisdictions.

**Legal Confirmation Required:** The interaction between EU ETS 2 and existing national systems (German National ETS, Austrian National ETS) once EU ETS 2 enters force — specifically whether national systems are phased out, merged, or run in parallel — is not resolved in the ICAP source data and requires monitoring of EU legislative instruments.

*Source: ICAP ETS Map — EU ETS 2 entry, ICAP, 2025–2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets.*

---

**MONITORING** — **Brazilian GHG ETS:** Under development. Covers maritime, domestic aviation, transport, industry, power, and other sectors. No start date published in available ICAP data. Brazil is a significant Americas trade lane for the workspace's luxury goods and live events verticals. When the Brazilian ETS enters force, it will add a carbon cost layer on Brazil-origin/destination ocean and road movements.

*Source: ICAP ETS Map — Brazilian GHG ETS entry, ICAP, 2025–2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets.*

---

**MONITORING** — **New York Cap-and-Invest (NYCI):** Under development. Covers maritime, waste, transport, buildings, industry, and power. No start date published in available ICAP data. New York is a major hub for live events, fine art, luxury goods, and film and TV cargo for this workspace profile. When enacted, NYCI will add a transport-fuel carbon cost for road carriers in New York State, with pass-through implications for drayage and last-mile on the workspace's most active US destination market.

*Source: ICAP ETS Map — USA New York NYCI entry, ICAP, 2025–2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets.*

---

**MONITORING** — **India Carbon Credit Trading Scheme:** In force from 2025. Covers industry. No published allowance price in available ICAP data. Effect on the workspace's India-routed air and road movements requires carrier-specific data as pricing data becomes available.

*Source: ICAP ETS Map — Indian Carbon Credit Trading Scheme entry, ICAP, 2025–2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets.*

---

**MONITORING** — **Turkey ETS:** Under development. No start date published in available ICAP data. Turkey is a transit and origin/destination market on the workspace's Europe trade lane. When enacted, will affect road and potentially air carrier costs on Turkey corridors.

*Source: ICAP ETS Map — Turkish ETS entry, ICAP, 2025–2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets.*

---

# Threshold Questions

| Question | Status |
|---|---|
| Does the workspace hold any direct ETS surrender, registration, or MRV obligation under any listed system as a freight forwarder? | **Legal Confirmation Required.** ICAP source data shows compliance obligations sitting with vessel operators, aircraft operators, and fuel distributors. No forwarder-specific obligation is documented, but this should be confirmed jurisdiction by jurisdiction with legal counsel. |
| Are any of the workspace's cargo verticals (live events, fine art, luxury goods, film/TV, high-value automotive, humanitarian) subject to sector-specific ETS treatment distinct from the carrier's obligation? | **Legal Confirmation Required.** No cargo-vertical-specific ETS treatment is documented in the ICAP source data. Cargo classification questions should be confirmed with legal counsel. |
| Does the workspace's role as freight forwarder in any jurisdiction trigger CBAM-adjacent or ETS-linked carbon reporting requirements as part of customs declarations? | **Legal Confirmation Required.** CBAM (EU Regulation 2023/956) applies to importers of covered goods; interaction with EU ETS for freight forwarders acting as customs declarant requires separate analysis not addressed in the ICAP ETS Map source. See CBAM Regulation (EU) 2023/956. |
| When carriers pass through EU ETS costs as surcharges, does the workspace have an obligation to disclose these as carbon-related costs in client invoices? | **Legal Confirmation Required.** No such obligation is documented in the ICAP ETS Map source. Check applicable national consumer/commercial disclosure rules. |

---

# Registration and Reporting Obligations

The ICAP ETS Map documents compliance obligations at the carrier/operator level. No registration or reporting obligation for freight forwarders as a distinct class is documented in the available source data.

**Gap:** Whether any ETS jurisdiction imposes indirect registration or supply-chain disclosure requirements on freight forwarders — as distinct from the carrier compliance obligation — is not addressed in the ICAP source. **Legal Confirmation Required** before concluding no registration obligation exists in any jurisdiction.

The workspace should note that the EU ETS requires aircraft operators to hold an EU ETS permit and surrender allowances, and vessel operators to surrender allowances under the maritime extension. The workspace's role is to receive and optionally disclose the cost pass-through from these operators.

*Source: ICAP ETS Map, ICAP, 2025–2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets.*

---

# Operational System Requirements

## Carbon Cost Visibility Infrastructure

**Scope:** Across all transport modes and trade lanes.
**Requirement (commercial, not regulatory):** To manage, report, and negotiate ETS pass-through costs, the workspace needs carrier invoice line-item data distinguishing ETS surcharges from base freight rates.
**Gap from current operational baseline:** Not assessable without internal systems data, but structurally: if carrier invoices do not separate ETS costs, the workspace cannot report them to clients, cannot benchmark them against market ETS prices, and cannot identify overcharging.
**Action:** Implement a carrier invoice review protocol that flags ETS line items. For carriers that do not itemise, request confirmation in writing.

## Carbon Price Monitoring Desk

**Scope:** EU ETS (air + maritime + industrial), UK ETS, California Cap-and-Trade, Washington Cap-and-Invest, Korea K-ETS, Japan GX-ETS — all covering modes the workspace uses.
**Requirement (commercial):** Monitor allowance prices quarterly. At EUR 74.35/tonne (EU ETS) and USD 61.36/tonne (Washington), price movements of 10–20% materially affect carrier surcharge levels and, downstream, the workspace's cost base on Europe and Pacific Northwest lanes.
**Action:** Subscribe to ICAP Allowance Price Explorer (https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets-prices) for live price tracking across all relevant systems.

*Source: ICAP Allowance Price Explorer, ICAP. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets-prices.*

## Developing-System Pipeline Watch

**Scope:** EU ETS 2, New York NYCI, Brazilian ETS, Turkey ETS.
**Requirement (forward planning):** Each of these systems covers transport in markets central to the workspace's operations. Entering force without advance notice will create unbudgeted cost increases for road and ocean movements in those markets.
**Action:** Assign responsibility within the operations or commercial team to track each system's legislative progress annually. ICAP ETS Map factsheets are updated regularly and provide the earliest public signal of implementation dates.

---

# Confirmed Regulatory Timeline

| Date | System | Status | Implication for the Workspace |
|---|---|---|---|
| 2005 | EU ETS (power/industry) | In force | Background; industrial emissions, not directly freight-relevant |
| 2012 | EU ETS aviation (intra-EEA) | In force | Air surcharges on Europe lanes: current cost |
| 2013 | California Cap-and-Trade | In force | Road/ocean carrier cost pass-through in California: current cost. Price: USD 29.10 (2025) |
| 2013 | Québec Cap-and-Trade | In force | Road carrier cost pass-through in Québec: current cost. Price: CAD 48.61 (2025) |
| 2015 | Korea K-ETS | In force | Air/road/ocean carrier pass-through on Korea lanes: current cost. Price: USD 6.60 (2025) |
| 2021 | UK ETS | In force | Air + maritime + industrial. Carrier pass-through on UK routes: current cost. Price: GBP 50.34 (2025) |
| 2021 | Germany National ETS | In force | Road transport fuel covered. Carrier pass-through in Germany: current cost. Price: EUR 55 fixed (2025) |
| 2022 | Austria National ETS | In force | Road transport fuel covered. Carrier pass-through in Austria: current cost. Price: EUR 55 fixed through 2027 |
| 2023 | Washington Cap-and-Invest | In force | Road + transport covered. Highest sub-national Americas price: USD 61.36 (2025). Current cost on Pacific Northwest road movements |
| 2024 | EU ETS Maritime (Phase 1, 50%) | In force | Ocean carrier pass-through to EU ports: current cost. Price: EUR 74.35 (2025) |
| 2025 | Japan GX-ETS | In force | Air, maritime, transport covered. Pass-through on Japan lanes: current cost. Price: not published in available data |
| 2025 | India Carbon Credit Trading Scheme | In force | Industry scope. Price: not published in available data |
| 2026 | Germany National ETS | Transitioning | Fixed price (EUR 55) gives way to auctioning with price corridor. Cost to road carriers in Germany will become market-driven. Monitor |
| 2026 | EU ETS Maritime (Phase 2 — 100%) | In force (expected phase schedule) | Ocean carrier pass-through increases to 100% of covered voyage emissions. Cost increase expected for EU-port ocean shipments. **COST ALERT** |
| TBD | EU ETS 2 (road transport) | Under development | Will add road transport fuel carbon cost across all EU/EEA states. Interaction with Germany/Austria national systems: **Legal Confirmation Required** |
| TBD | New York NYCI | Under development | Will add transport carbon cost in New York — key market for live events, fine art, luxury goods, film and TV |
| TBD | Brazilian ETS | Under development | Will add maritime + transport carbon cost on Brazil trade lanes |
| TBD | Turkey ETS | Under development | Will affect carrier costs on Turkey corridors within Europe trade lane |

*Source: All timeline entries derived from ICAP ETS Map, ICAP, 2025–2026. https://icapcarbonaction.com/en/ets.*
